target.title: feat(memory): carry source actor context into recall
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Memory recall can currently receive the conversation/session context without a stable indication of which group participant authored the triggering message. In multi-person channels, that can make retrieved or promoted memory read as if another participant's facts belong to the operator. This PR carries the generic source actor context into memory recall and prompt supplements so memory backends can preserve participant attribution without adding backend-specific logic.

## Summary

- Carries `SourceActor` from inbound context through embedded runs and plugin tool context.
- Exposes the same source actor context to memory corpus supplements for `memory_search` and `memory_get`.
- Adds memory prompt/flush guidance to preserve participant attribution instead of merging another participant's facts into the operator/user profile.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738, `codex/signal-mention-facts-context`, and `codex/source-actor-context-contract`. Until those land, this PR's diff will include its dependencies.

## Production impact

No storage migration and no backend-specific behavior. This is not Honcho-specific: it adds a generic runtime/tool/memory contract that memory backends and supplements can consume. Core memory search still filters by existing session/source visibility; this PR does not claim a new per-person storage partition.

## Evidence

- Agent/tool context tests cover `SourceActor` propagation into plugin tool context.
- Auto-reply and inbound metadata tests cover carrying source actor context through reply runs.
- Memory-core tests cover source actor supplements in recall/citation paths and attribution guidance in memory output.
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:

target.title: fix(codex): honor workspace-only fs for native tools
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
When an OpenClaw agent sets `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`, OpenClaw-owned file tools enforce that boundary. The Codex app-server native tool surface can bypass that policy if left enabled, because native Codex tools are not constrained by OpenClaw's workspace-only fs wrapper. This disables the native tool surface for the effective workspace-only fs policy while leaving non-workspace-only agents unchanged.

## Summary
- disable Codex app-server native tool surfaces when effective OpenClaw fs policy is workspace-only
- resolve the policy from the active agent, runtime/session key, default agent, then global config
- reuse OpenClaw's exported config/routing helpers for policy lookup
- cover mixed-agent, global, default-agent, runtime-session, explicit per-agent opt-out, and malformed-config cases

## Evidence
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts` passed locally: 1 file, 55 tests
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed locally
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Notes
- Per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` is treated as an explicit opt-out from a global workspace-only policy; omit the per-agent key to fall through to the global policy.
- Full repo extension typecheck currently fails on unrelated `acpx` and `qa-lab` / `@openclaw/crabline` errors before this patch-specific surface can be isolated.

target.title: fix(terminal-core): strip controls from terminal links
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`formatTerminalLink()` only removed ESC from OSC-8 labels and URLs. Other terminal control characters, especially BEL (`\^G`), could still be embedded in the user-provided URL or label. In forced OSC output, BEL terminates the OSC sequence, so an input URL could prematurely end the hyperlink URL segment and leak the remaining text into the terminal output structure.

## Why This Change Was Made

The formatter now strips C0/C1 terminal control characters from both label and URL before building either OSC-8 output or plain fallback output. This preserves the existing behavior of dropping ESC while covering the delimiter used by the generated OSC sequence.

A focused test covers both forced OSC output and fallback output.

## User Impact

Terminal links remain readable, but untrusted label/URL text can no longer inject control characters into the OSC-8 hyperlink structure.

## Evidence

Direct behavior probe:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./packages/terminal-core/src/terminal-link.ts').then(({ formatTerminalLink }) => { const out = formatTerminalLink('safe\^Glabel', 'https://example.test/a\^Goops', { force: true }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ out, containsInjectedDelimiter: out.includes('\^Goops') })); })"
{"out":"\^[]8;;https://example.test/aoops\^Gsafelabel\^[]8;;\^G","containsInjectedDelimiter":false}
```

Targeted test:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/terminal-core/src/terminal-link.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Start at  21:20:48

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.48s
```



target.title: feat(channels): expose source actor attribution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Group-channel messages need a generic way to distinguish the runtime message author from the operator/user represented by the agent session. Without source actor attribution, downstream prompts, tools, and memory adapters can only see channel/session context and may conflate another group participant with the operator. This should be solved as a channel contract, not as a deployment-specific memory backend workaround.

## Summary

- Adds a generic `SourceActor` inbound context contract for the runtime identity of the message author.
- Maps Signal sender identity into `SourceActor` without making the contract Signal-specific.
- Documents that channel plugins should provide source actor attribution generically rather than hard-coding deployment-specific people or memory backends.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738 and `codex/signal-mention-facts-context`. Until those land, this PR's diff will include its dependencies.

## Production impact

No behavior change by itself. The change gives prompts, tools, and memory integrations a stable way to distinguish a group participant from the operator/user.

## Evidence

- Channel context tests cover generic `SourceActor` projection.
- Signal mention-gating tests cover Signal sender identity mapping into the generic context.
- Plugin SDK runtime mock tests cover `SourceActor*` parity with production inbound context.

target.title: fix(signal): expose mention facts in inbound context
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal group mention gating now has native mention metadata available, but the decision is only visible inside the Signal monitor. Downstream prompt/tool context cannot inspect whether the inbound group message had native mention metadata, whether `requireMention` was enforced, or why a message was skipped. That makes native-only group behavior hard to debug and forces consumers to infer channel-specific facts from text.

## Summary

- Exposes Signal mention-gating facts on the generic inbound message context.
- Adds `CanDetectMention`, `HasAnyMention`, `RequireMention`, `EffectiveWasMentioned`, `MentionShouldSkip`, and `MentionSource` for native/text mention decisions.
- Keeps the Signal mention source literal typed so SDK/context consumers can rely on `"native"` vs `"mention_pattern"`.

## Stack

This is stacked after #96738 (`fix(signal): detect native bot mentions in group gating`). Until #96738 lands, this PR's diff will include that dependency.

## Production impact

No new outbound behavior. This only makes already-computed mention gating facts inspectable by prompts, tests, and plugin/channel contracts.

## Evidence

- Focused Signal/channel tests cover native mention facts being projected into inbound context:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Broader affected-context regression suite passed:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.mention-gating.test.ts`
- Production TypeScript check passed:
  `pnpm tsgo:prod`

### Redacted Runtime Proof

Temporary local proof harness imported production `buildChannelInboundEventContext` and printed the emitted prompt context on PR head `23900c0b0680`.


target.title: fix(ios): reset sidebar navigation stacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the iPad/sidebar navigation regression where a pushed Settings detail could survive after selecting another sidebar root such as Overview, Chat, or Talk.
- Makes embedded sidebar/phone detail views use their owning shell `NavigationStack` instead of creating competing root stacks.
- Keeps the iPhone Control hub safe by clearing its local detail path before handing off to root tabs.
- Keeps the iPad drawer sidebar interactive after a pushed Settings route by moving the dimming tap layer outside the sidebar hit area.
- Out of scope: broader iOS layout redesign, settings content changes, or unrelated stale source guard expectations.

Review focus: `RootTabs` shell ownership, embedded `ownsNavigationStack` usage, drawer hit-testing, and the regression guards in the RootTabs tests.

## Linked context

Closes: none

Related: none

Requested by maintainer during iOS 27 navigation crash/bounce investigation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: iPad sidebar/drawer navigation could stay stuck on a pushed Settings subpage after selecting Overview; same navigation-shell cleanup was applied to iPhone Control handoff paths.
- Real environment tested: local iOS Simulator via XcodeBuildMCP, iPad profile `OpenClaw iPad Preview` and iPhone 17 Pro profile, screenshot fixture mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: launched the app with `--openclaw-screenshot-mode --openclaw-initial-destination settings --openclaw-sidebar-visibility visible`, opened Settings -> Channels / Integrations, opened the sidebar drawer, then tapped Overview.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

After fix, tapping Overview exits the pushed Channels detail and shows the Overview screen:

![After: Settings subpage switches to Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw after recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

After runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/after-ipad-runtime.log

Physical iOS 27 device install/connect proof captured after the fix:

```text
openclaw nodes status --json
nodeId: cf74bea339636ed9cee784f41e00f650afba207c22ef0efd5045aa2618ed5454
platform: iOS 27.0.0
version: 2026.6.2
clientMode: node
remoteIp: 192.168.1.231
approvalState: approved
paired: true
connected: true
lastSeenReason: connect
caps: 11
commands: 14

openclaw devices list --json
deviceId: cf74bea339636ed9cee784f41e00f650afba207c22ef0efd5045aa2618ed5454
platform: iOS 27.0.0
clientMode: node
role: node
roles: node, operator
tokenRoles: node, operator
lastSeenReason: connect
```

Physical-device runtime note: the gateway log also captured the earlier setup repair failures from the same phone (`token_missing`, `bootstrap_token_invalid`, then `token_mismatch` while stale credentials were being cleared). After reset/re-pair, the current node state above is connected/approved.

- Observed result after fix: semantic UI snapshot after tapping Overview contained `Overview`, `Gateway Healthy`, and no longer showed the Channels detail as the active screen.
- What was not tested: physical iOS 27 install/connect/log proof is now captured; the original Overview bounce/crash tap path is still covered by simulator before/after GIFs rather than a physical-device video.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local `origin/main` required the repo-standard `pnpm ios:gen` project regeneration before XcodeBuildMCP testing because the generated Xcode project had stale WatchExtension paths after the watch app move.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Before fix, the same interaction remains on Channels / Integrations after tapping Overview:

![Before: Settings subpage remains stale after Overview tap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw before recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

Before runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/before-ipad-runtime.log

Proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/proof-summary.md

## Tests and validation

Commands/proof run:

- `pnpm ios:gen`
- XcodeBuildMCP iPad focused tests: `-only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsSidebarRegressionTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsPresentationTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/SwiftUIRenderSmokeTests` -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- XcodeBuildMCP iPhone focused tests: same focused suite -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Settings embedded under a parent navigation stack.
- Sidebar selection clears the Settings route path before swapping roots.
- iPad drawer dimming layer does not steal sidebar touches.
- iPhone Control hub clears its detail path before root-tab handoff.

Known existing failures/gaps:

- Full `RootTabsSourceGuardTests` had pre-existing stale gateway transport string guard failures during earlier local checks; the touched focused suites above pass.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Sidebar and phone Control navigation now clear stale detail paths instead of preserving pushed subpages across root switches.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

SwiftUI navigation ownership across iPad sidebar, iPhone Control hub, and standalone tab contexts.

How is that risk mitigated?

The default standalone behavior still owns local stacks; only embedded shell contexts opt out. Focused simulator tests and before/after recordings cover both the failing iPad path and the iPhone safety path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. Physical iOS 27 install/connect proof is captured above; physical-device video of the Overview tap path was not captured.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the review concern about stale Settings detail screens by binding/resetting the sidebar navigation path, and expanded the fix after live proof showed the drawer dimming layer could still intercept sidebar selection.

AI-assisted: Yes. Local Codex/autoreview was run and returned no accepted/actionable findings.

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so Telegram/forum reply facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata.

This is the Morty patch shape, polished for upstream:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty
- keeps replied-to body text out of the trusted metadata block and verbose trace
- folds in the hidden runtime-context routing from #89428 so current inbound/reply metadata no longer gets prepended into `promptForSession` / `promptForModel`

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Builds on the same routing direction as #89428. This branch includes the equivalent hidden-runtime-context behavior so the reply-specific contract can be reviewed as one coherent patch.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can carry `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the agent runtime needs a structured current-turn reply contract that stays available to the model without being persisted as user-authored prompt text.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`. The proof uses redacted Telegram/forum-style ids.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** First, built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the current-turn runtime prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

Then, exercised the hidden runtime-context routing with the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper and verified that reply metadata stays out of the submitted/persisted prompt strings while remaining in `runtimeContext`.

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF'
import { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts';
const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({
  effectivePrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  transcriptPrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  currentInboundContext: {
    text: 'Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=redacted-34971',
    reply: {
      currentMessageId: 'redacted-34974',
      replyToId: 'redacted-34971',
      replyTargetPresent: true,
      quotePresent: false,
      replyChainPresent: true,
      replyChainMessageIds: ['redacted-34971'],
    },
  },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  prompt: parts.prompt,
  modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt,
  promptContainsReplyMetadata: parts.prompt.includes('Current reply metadata') || parts.prompt.includes('redacted-34971'),
  modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata: (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('Current reply metadata') || (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('redacted-34971'),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('Current reply metadata')),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyToId: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('redacted-34971')),
}, null, 2));
PROOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the first real module command:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": ["34971"]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

Console output from the hidden runtime-context proof:

```json
{
  "prompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "modelPrompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, omitted from the visible/model prompt strings, and delivered through hidden runtime context. The trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.


**Live Telegram/forum transport proof (redacted):** A real Telegram forum reply was sent in an existing group topic on 2026-06-05 UTC. No live OpenClaw install or runtime was changed for this transport proof.

Redacted inbound metadata supplied to the agent turn:

```json
{
  "channel": "telegram",
  "surface": "telegram",
  "chat_type": "group",
  "is_forum": true,
  "topic_id": "<redacted-topic>",
  "message_id": "<redacted-current>",
  "reply_to_id": "<redacted-anchor>",
  "was_mentioned": true,
  "has_reply_context": true
}
```

The assembled current message included a Telegram reply target for the proof anchor while the persisted session user message remained the user-authored text only:

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "sourceChannel": "telegram",
  "content": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745"
}
```

Then the PR branch was exercised with the same redacted Telegram/forum shape through the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper:

```json
{
  "prompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "modelPrompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsForumSignal": true
}
```

This pairs a real Telegram/forum reply event with the PR-branch runtime handoff: the transport supplies `reply_to_id`/forum/mention facts, the session prompt remains clean user text, and the PR branch keeps reply/forum metadata in hidden runtime context instead of visible/model prompt text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against this PR branch, because Sean's live OpenClaw runtime was intentionally not changed.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 36 tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - passed on 2026-06-15 after the embedded-attempt handoff repair and rebase onto current main; 3 Vitest shards, 98 tests
- `git diff --check` - passed on 2026-06-15

target.title: fix(msteams): read file attachments on Teams channel messages (team GUID + channel fallback + thread-reply URL)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes inbound **file attachments on Microsoft Teams channel messages** (`@openclaw/msteams`). Three independent bugs meant the agent could never read a file attached in a channel — and for thread replies it silently returned the *thread root's* file instead. Detailed root-cause write-up in #90733 (closed as a duplicate of this issue). All three confirmed present on `main` and v2026.6.1.

### 1. Wrong team id → Graph `400`
`buildMSTeamsGraphMessageUrls` built `/teams/{team-id}/…` from `channelData.team.id`, which is the `19:…@thread.tacv2` thread id — not the team's AAD group GUID. The Graph `/teams/{id}` path needs `channelData.team.aadGroupId`. Now prefers `aadGroupId` (with the old fields as fallback).

### 2. Graph fetch never attempted for channel @mentions
The fetch was gated on `hasHtmlFileAttachment` (an `<attachment id>` HTML marker). Channel @mention activities deliver only a `text/html` stub without that marker, so the fetch was skipped even though the file is fetchable via Graph. Now attempts the Graph message fetch for non-personal conversations regardless of the marker.

### 3. Thread replies fetched the wrong message
A reply was fetched at `/messages/{replyId}` (404 — a reply id is not a top-level message) then fell back to `/messages/{root}` (the thread root), returning the **root's** attachment for every reply. A reply is addressable only at `/messages/{root}/replies/{replyId}`. Now treats any candidate that isn't the current message id as a potential parent, builds the reply URL first, and only fetches `/messages/{selfId}` for the message itself — never the bare root.

### Changes
- `extensions/msteams/src/attachments/graph.ts` — team GUID resolution; channel reply-URL construction.
- `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/inbound-media.ts` — attempt the channel Graph fetch without an HTML marker.
- `extensions/msteams/src/attachments.helpers.test.ts` — regression test (aadGroupId + reply-under-root, never the bare root).
- `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/inbound-media.test.ts` — the mention-only case now expects the Graph fallback for channel/group threads.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reading file attachments (PDF + audio) from Microsoft Teams channel messages — top-level and thread replies. Before the fix the agent reported "no attachment" on top-level messages and returned the thread root's file on replies.

Real environment tested: a live Microsoft Teams tenant on a deployed OpenClaw agent, plus direct Microsoft Graph calls with the bot app token (Graph application permission `ChannelMessage.Read.All` admin-consented; manifest RSC `ChannelMessage.Read.Group`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: @mentioned the bot in a channel with a file attached on (1) a top-level message and (2) a thread reply, for both a PDF and an mp3 — the agent fetched and read/transcribed the correct file each time. Also issued the four Graph calls shown below directly with the bot token to confirm the URL shapes.

Evidence after fix: redacted runtime console output from the live agent's plugin (a debug probe added during diagnosis), for a PDF attached as a thread reply — the corrected reply URL is built first and Graph returns the reply's own attachment (`200`, `attachmentCount:1`):

```
[RADAR-DBG] ids {"selfId":"1780683115056","conversationMessageId":"1780681398985","parents":["1780681398985"]}
[RADAR-DBG] graphUrls ["https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teams/<aadGroupId>/channels/<chan>/messages/1780681398985/replies/1780683115056",
                       "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teams/<aadGroupId>/channels/<chan>/messages/1780683115056"]
[RADAR-DBG] graphMedia {"mediaLen":1,"attempts":[{"url":".../messages/1780681398985/replies/1780683115056","hostedStatus":200,"attachmentStatus":200,"attachmentCount":1}]}
```

Direct Microsoft Graph calls with the bot app token, confirming each URL shape (redacted host/ids):

```
GET /teams/{team.id == 19:..@thread.tacv2}/channels/{c}/messages/{id}     -> 400  (invalid team id)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{topLevelId}                -> 200  (+ attachment)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{replyId}                   -> 404  (reply is not a top-level message)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{rootId}/replies/{replyId}  -> 200  (+ the reply's actual attachment)
```

Observed result after fix: the correct file is read every time — for replies and top-level, documents and audio. A top-level PDF, a thread-reply PDF, and a thread-reply mp3 were each read/transcribed correctly; previously all returned the thread-root file.

What was not tested: the local `vitest` / `oxfmt` / `tsgo` suite was not run on my machine (developed against a release checkout; behavior verified live and via direct Graph calls instead). Please confirm CI — happy to adjust to maintainer preference.

Fixes #89594

target.title: feat(hooks): add Gmail Pub/Sub pull delivery mode
target.body:
# Human-written request

i think you should now start drafting implementation for each of the tools in worktrees and then opening draft PRs against the repos, at least for gog and openclaw. can you do that using [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) ?

# Draft scope

RFC: openclaw/rfcs#8
Requires companion gog PR: openclaw/gogcli#700
Current companion gog head: `86975b69542c35c814ed3075275ecccc735c5fd6`

This draft wires OpenClaw to supervise a Gmail Pub/Sub pull delivery runner once gog exposes `gog gmail watch pull`.

Merge sequencing: this OpenClaw PR should stay draft/blocked until the companion gog PR lands, or until the OpenClaw runtime/package path otherwise guarantees a `gog` binary containing `gog gmail watch pull`.

# What changed

- Adds `hooks.gmail.delivery.mode` with `push` and `pull` variants.
- Keeps omitted `delivery.mode` as the existing push behavior for compatibility.
- Requires pull delivery to use a full Pub/Sub subscription path: `projects/<project>/subscriptions/<subscription>`.
- Starts `gog gmail watch pull` for pull configs and `gog gmail watch serve` for push configs.
- Keeps Tailscale setup, push-token handling, bind/port/path, and address-in-use checks scoped to push mode.
- Updates config schema, labels, help quality tests, docs, and CLI descriptions so setup remains push-oriented while run/gateway supervision become delivery-mode aware.

# Compatibility

- Existing push deployments stay on push when `delivery.mode` is omitted.
- `openclaw webhooks gmail setup` remains push-oriented in this draft; pull-mode cloud setup is a later slice after gog pull support is merged and dogfooded.
- This does not change hook payload schema.
- This depends on `openclaw/gogcli#700`; without that command in the runtime PATH, pull configs will fail at watcher spawn time.

# Testing strategy

Already run in this draft:

- `nix shell nixpkgs#nodejs_24 --command node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/gmail.test.ts src/hooks/gmail-watcher.test.ts src/cli/webhooks-cli.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Deterministic coverage added here:

- legacy Gmail configs without `delivery.mode` still resolve as push and keep push token/serve defaults;
- pull configs resolve without push token, serve, or Tailscale settings;
- pull configs require full Pub/Sub subscription paths;
- pull log args redact hook tokens;
- watcher startup spawns `gog gmail watch pull` with the expected account, subscription, and hook flags;
- pull watcher startup does not run Tailscale setup.

Remote proof completed for this draft head:

- Manual OpenClaw `CI` workflow dispatch https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27035870855 completed successfully for commit `89fcda4a6419bf3ebc2141819f11be99eceaee0c`.
- That remote run covered the broad OpenClaw CI matrix, including `check-test-types`, `check-lint`, docs, dependency/guard shards, build artifacts, Node 22 compatibility, Windows Node tests, macOS Node tests, macOS Swift build/test, and the relevant Node test shards.

target.title: fix(tasks): serialize structured task values for sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- serialize structured `TaskRecord.task` values before binding them into sqlite
- preserve existing string task values unchanged
- add regression coverage that object task payloads are stored as JSON strings instead of throwing during sqlite binding

## Root cause

The sqlite task registry store binds `record.task` directly into the `task_runs.task` column. Most task values are strings, but structured task payloads can appear from agent/runtime paths. Passing a non-string object directly to sqlite binding can throw or persist incorrectly. The store should normalize the value before binding.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/tasks/task-registry.store.sqlite.ts src/tasks/task-registry.store.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Support compat reasoning levels for thinking xhigh
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Allows OpenAI-compatible reasoning models to expose `xhigh` thinking when the model configuration explicitly declares API support through `compat.supportedReasoningEfforts`.
- Keeps `xhigh` hidden for reasoning models that do not declare extended compatible reasoning effort support.
- Keeps `max` conservative: it is surfaced only when compat metadata explicitly supports `max` (for example `reasoningEffortMap.max`), while plain `supportedReasoningEfforts: ["xhigh"]` surfaces only `xhigh`.

Why does this matter now?

- Some OpenAI-compatible providers support reasoning efforts beyond OpenAI's standard `minimal`/`low`/`medium`/`high` set, but OpenClaw's UI/runtime capability calculation filtered those levels out before consulting explicit compat support.
- That makes valid configured models look like they cannot use `xhigh`, even when the provider explicitly lists that effort in compat metadata.

What is the intended outcome?

- Configured compatible models can advertise and use `xhigh` only when `compat.supportedReasoningEfforts` explicitly includes it, and can advertise `max` only when explicit compat metadata says `max` is supported.
- Default reasoning-model behavior remains conservative for providers without explicit extended effort support.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No new config fields.
- No change to model definitions that do not opt into extended compatible reasoning efforts.
- No local installed-runtime hotfix; this is the durable source-level change.

What does success look like?

- Existing OpenAI reasoning models keep the previous supported thinking levels.
- OpenAI-compatible models with explicit `supportedReasoningEfforts` can surface `xhigh`; `reasoningEffortMap` can map supported levels but does not by itself expose new extended levels; `max` requires explicit `max` support in compat metadata.
- OpenAI-compatible models without explicit extended effort support still do not surface `xhigh`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The compatibility gate around extended reasoning efforts.
- Whether `max` is gated strictly enough for compatible-provider reasoning effort checks.
- Regression coverage in `src/llm/model-utils.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #59416
Related #74273
Related #84902

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request from me; this is an external contributor fix for behavior observed in a real OpenClaw runtime using an OpenAI-compatible reasoning model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI-compatible reasoning models with explicit compat metadata for extended reasoning efforts were not allowed to expose `xhigh` thinking, and `max` needed an explicit opt-in path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime on Linux, current session using model `codex-lb-2455/gpt-5.5` with `Think: xhigh` visible in `session_status` output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `session_status(model="", sessionKey="current")`
  - Focused source tests and changed-file checks listed below.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - `session_status` output included: `OpenClaw 2026.6.1`, `Model: codex-lb-2455/gpt-5.5`, and `Think: xhigh`.
  - Targeted regression tests in `src/llm/model-utils.test.ts` pass locally.
- Observed result after fix: Compatible reasoning-level capability calculation now includes `xhigh` for explicitly opted-in compatible models, includes `max` only with explicit `max` support, and keeps non-opted-in models conservative.
- What was not tested: The repository's delegated Blacksmith Testbox changed-file gate could not complete from this host because the Blacksmith CLI is not authenticated to an organization.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith browser auth completed to a `No Organizations Found` page because this GitHub account has no organization with the Blacksmith GitHub App installed. `blacksmith auth status` reports `Not authenticated to any organization. Run 'blacksmith auth login' to get started.`
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The original runtime issue was that `xhigh` was not surfaced for an OpenAI-compatible reasoning model even though compat metadata mapped/supported the provider effort.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/llm/model-utils.ts src/llm/model-utils.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint src/llm/model-utils.ts src/llm/model-utils.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/model-utils.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:changed -- src/llm/model-utils.ts src/llm/model-utils.test.ts
blacksmith auth status || true
```

Results:

- `oxfmt`: passed, all matched files use the correct format.
- `oxlint`: passed, 0 warnings and 0 errors.
- Targeted Vitest: passed, `src/llm/model-utils.test.ts`, 4 tests / 4 passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`: passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`: passed.
- `pnpm check:changed`: blocked by Blacksmith CLI auth/org infrastructure, not by a code/test failure:

```text
[check:changed] delegating to Blacksmith Testbox via `pnpm crabbox:run`.
[crabbox] bin=../crabbox/bin/crabbox version=0.26.0-1-gc6fef7c provider=blacksmith-testbox providers=apple-container,ascii-box,aws,azure,azure-dynamic-sessions,blacksmith-testbox,cloudflare,daytona,e2b,exe-dev,gcp,hetzner,islo,local-container,modal,multipass,namespace-devbox,parallels,proxmox,railway,runpod,semaphore,sprites,ssh,tensorlake,upstash-box,wandb
Error: not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first
not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first
blacksmith testbox warmup failed: blacksmith failed: exit status 1; if the delegated queue is unavailable, rerun with a coordinator-backed provider such as --provider aws
```

`blacksmith auth status`:

```text
Not authenticated to any organization. Run 'blacksmith auth login' to get started.
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added tests for OpenAI-compatible reasoning models that explicitly support `xhigh` via `compat.supportedReasoningEfforts`.
- Added tests for conservative `compat.reasoningEffortMap` / `max` alias behavior, including map-only aliases that must not expose new extended levels.
- Added tests that reasoning models without explicit extended compat support still do not expose `xhigh`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Capability calculation filtered out `xhigh`/`max` for compatible reasoning models before explicit compat reasoning effort support was considered; the follow-up also prevents map-only aliases from exposing new extended levels.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, for explicitly configured OpenAI-compatible reasoning models only.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally exposing unsupported thinking levels for providers that do not really accept extended reasoning efforts.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The new behavior is gated on explicit compat support (`supportedReasoningEfforts` / `reasoningEffortMap`).
- Regression tests cover both opted-in and non-opted-in reasoning models.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review. GitHub CI is green; delegated local Blacksmith Testbox validation remains unavailable in this external contributor environment.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- The external contributor environment cannot complete the delegated `pnpm check:changed` Blacksmith Testbox gate because Blacksmith auth has no available organization. GitHub CI for the PR is green.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- claw-sweeper's `max`/`xhigh` compatibility concern was addressed in follow-up commit `e8292687` by requiring explicit `max` compat metadata before surfacing `max`.

target.title: fix(memory-core): denoise dreaming wrappers and promote grounded REM truths
target.body:
## Summary
This PR finishes the second half of the Dreaming denoise work and unblocks deep promotion.

It does three things:
- strips wrapper/prompt scaffolding earlier during session text extraction
- suppresses meta-language in REM (`assistant`, `report`, `candidate`, etc.) while biasing toward project-grade truths
- feeds REM candidate truths back into grounded short-term candidates so deep promotion can actually pass

## What changed

### A. Strip session wrapper pollution earlier
Filtered from session extraction / corpus ingestion:
- `<relevant-memories>...</relevant-memories>`
- cron task envelopes
- `Current time:`
- `NO_REPLY` / `SKIPPED`
- startup/reset bootstrap text
- async exec receipts (`System (untrusted): ...`)

### B. Denoise REM themes and truths
Added REM-side filtering for:
- meta snippets
- meta tags
- direct `Assistant:` / `User:` transcript lines

Added positive REM bias for project-like signals:
- decision
- policy
- workflow
- plan
- cadence
- project
- publish
- strategy

Added negative suppression for engineering-noise terms:
- build
- plugin
- warning
- smoke
- patch
- fix
- debug
- test

### C. Unblock deep promotion
Previously REM could produce better output, but deep still stayed at 0 because the candidate pipeline did not treat REM truths as strong enough promotion signals.

This PR:
- records REM candidate truths as grounded short-term candidates
- increases their effective signal strength
- lets grounded + phase reinforcement count toward deep gating/diversity
- adds grounded boost in ranking

## Validation
Observed progression during rebuild validation:
- session-corpus noise dropped substantially
- REM stopped being dominated by `assistant/user/report`
- REM started surfacing project-grade truths
- deep promotion moved from `passingCount=0` to `passingCount=3`

Example passing candidates after the final rebuild included:
- Operation_Undock narrative positioning
- Plot_Engine / OpenClaw Docker isolation status
- `gog gmail` guardrail decision

## Tests
Added or updated coverage for:
- wrapper stripping in session extraction
- concept-tag meta stopwords
- REM preview filtering
- transcript-line exclusion from lasting truths
- deep gating with reinforced grounded candidates

## Notes
- Full Vitest runs were expensive in this workspace and repeatedly hit plugin timing / process kill limits, so validation here relied on focused smoke checks plus repeated rebuild verification against real dreaming artifacts.

target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock): skip IMDS during credential auto-detection (#64891)
target.body:
﻿## Summary

- Stop Bedrock memory auto-detection from probing AWS IMDS (`169.254.169.254`) when no AWS credential signals are configured.
- Keep the AWS SDK default credential chain available for explicitly selected/configured Bedrock memory providers, including shared config/profile, SSO, web identity, ECS, and EC2/IMDS role setups.
- During non-IMDS profile/container/web-identity probes, temporarily set `AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED=true` so `defaultProvider()` skips instance metadata while still resolving configured non-IMDS sources.
- Memoize `process.env` credential probes for 5 minutes and coalesce concurrent calls without sharing cached results across IMDS-enabled and IMDS-disabled probe modes.

Closes #64891

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Bedrock memory auto-detection no longer loads the AWS SDK credential chain, and therefore does not reach IMDS, when there are no AWS credential signals. Explicit Bedrock memory configuration still allows the full AWS SDK default chain.

Real environment tested: Windows local OpenClaw source checkout, running the patched Amazon Bedrock provider code directly through Node 22/tsx with AWS credential environment variables cleared.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
node --import tsx -e "const awsKeys=['AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID','AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY','AWS_SESSION_TOKEN','AWS_PROFILE','AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK','AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI','AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_FULL_URI','AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE','AWS_ROLE_ARN','AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED']; for (const key of awsKeys) delete process.env[key]; const { hasAwsCredentials } = await import('./extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.ts'); const started=Date.now(); const result=await hasAwsCredentials(); console.log(JSON.stringify({setup:'local OpenClaw source checkout',command:'hasAwsCredentials() with AWS credential env cleared',awsCredentialEnvPresent:awsKeys.filter((key)=>process.env[key]),result,elapsedMs:Date.now()-started,metadataDisabledAfter:process.env.AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED ?? null},null,2));"
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the local OpenClaw source checkout:

```json
{
  "setup": "local OpenClaw source checkout",
  "command": "hasAwsCredentials() with AWS credential env cleared",
  "awsCredentialEnvPresent": [],
  "result": false,
  "elapsedMs": 0,
  "metadataDisabledAfter": null
}
```

Observed result after fix: With AWS credential environment cleared, the real provider code returned `false` immediately from the no-signal auto-detection path. The command completed without setting `AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED` and without waiting on the AWS SDK metadata timeout path.

What was not tested: A live AWS EC2/ECS/default-chain credential setup and the original non-AWS sandbox packet trace were not available in this environment; those remain maintainer/live-environment proof gaps.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/memory-embedding-adapter.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false`
- `pnpm exec tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json --pretty false`
- `OPENCLAW_EXTENSION_BOUNDARY_CONCURRENCY=1 pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `OPENCLAW_EXTENSION_BOUNDARY_CONCURRENCY=1 pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/memory-embedding-adapter.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/memory-embedding-adapter.test.ts`

## Test plan

- [x] Empty env skips credential SDK load
- [x] `AWS_EC2_METADATA_DISABLED=true` exits without SDK when no probe signals
- [x] `AWS_PROFILE` probe disables IMDS temporarily and restores env
- [x] Explicit Bedrock provider/config preserves full AWS SDK default chain probing

- [ ] Maintainer/live env: non-AWS sandbox startup shows no repeated `169.254.169.254` traffic
- [ ] Maintainer/live env: EC2/ECS/default-chain Bedrock memory credentials still resolve

target.title: fix(agents): align custom provider auth labels with runtime (#82020)
target.body:
﻿## Summary

- Fix `resolveModelAuthLabel` so `/status` and other UI surfaces show the same auth source as runtime resolution for custom providers like `zai-light`.
- Keep per-entry literal `apiKey` bindings labeled as `api-key (models.json)` when runtime also resolves that provider entry from `models.json`, while leaving the late generic custom-key fallback behind env/CLI labels.

Closes #82020

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/status` and other UI/status surfaces could show the wrong auth source for a custom provider when `zai` and `zai-light` share a base URL. The fix makes `resolveModelAuthLabel` agree with `resolveApiKeyForProvider`: the `zai-light` per-entry literal key is reported as `api-key (models.json)`, and generic late custom-key fallback no longer masks env labels.

Real environment tested: Local Windows checkout (`D:\WorkSpace\AI\openclaw`) with Node `v24.13.1` and pnpm `11.2.2`, using the PR branch `fix/provider-auth-label-82020` after the patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
pnpm exec tsx -e "(async()=>{const { resolveModelAuthLabel } = await import('./src/agents/model-auth-label.ts'); const { resolveApiKeyForProvider } = await import('./src/agents/model-auth.ts'); const cfg={models:{providers:{zai:{api:'openai-completions',baseUrl:'https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/coding/paas/v4',apiKey:'zai-api-key',models:[]},'zai-light':{api:'openai-completions',baseUrl:'https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/coding/paas/v4',apiKey:'sk-light-only',models:[{id:'glm-5-turbo',name:'glm-5-turbo',reasoning:false,input:['text'],cost:{input:0,output:0,cacheRead:0,cacheWrite:0},contextWindow:8192,maxTokens:4096}]}}}}; process.env.ZAI_API_KEY='zai-env-key'; const auth=await resolveApiKeyForProvider({provider:'zai-light',cfg}); const label=resolveModelAuthLabel({provider:'zai-light',cfg}); console.log(JSON.stringify({authSource:auth.source,authKey:auth.apiKey,label},null,2));})();"
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "authSource": "models.json",
  "authKey": "sk-light-only",
  "label": "api-key (models.json)"
}
```

Observed result after fix: With `ZAI_API_KEY` also set, runtime auth for `zai-light` resolves the literal per-entry `models.json` key and the status label reports `api-key (models.json)`, so the displayed auth source matches the credential source that runtime uses.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Telegram `/status` transcript. The visible Telegram behavior is covered by the shared `resolveModelAuthLabel` status helper plus the terminal runtime/label proof above; maintainers can still request Mantis Telegram proof if they want transport-visible confirmation.

## Tests

- `pnpm tsgo:test`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts -t "custom provider keys|late custom-key|literal per-entry" --reporter=dot`

## Test plan

- [x] Scoped Vitest regression for `zai-light` + shared `ZAI_API_KEY`
- [x] Terminal proof that runtime key and status label agree
- [x] Typecheck proof for test fixtures with `pnpm tsgo:test`
- [ ] Maintainer: Telegram `/status` on a config with `zai` + `zai-light` shows `api-key (models.json)` for the custom provider

target.title: fix(message): route WebChat message tool sends to source sink
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96840.

Targetless `message.send` calls created for WebChat source turns were still using automatic source delivery, so the outbound runner required an explicit target even though WebChat should provide the internal source-reply sink for message-tool sends.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps ordinary WebChat room-event turns on automatic source delivery, but defaults WebChat-constructed (internal-channel) message tools to `message_tool_only` unless a caller explicitly supplies another source delivery mode. That keeps the existing gateway routing behavior intact while giving targetless WebChat message-tool sends the internal sink they need.

## User Impact

WebChat agents can call `message.send` without an explicit target when replying to the current source, matching the documented/source-backed behavior. Explicit automatic source delivery remains preserved for callers that request it.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Targetless `message(action="send")` from an active WebChat run failed with `Action send requires a target` instead of being projected into the active chat via the internal source-reply sink (#96840, per `docs/web/webchat.md`). After this patch, internal-channel (WebChat) message tools default `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` to `message_tool_only`, so a targetless send routes through the internal sink.

**Real environment tested:** Local `openclaw` source checkout in isolated git worktrees — PR branch `oss/96840-webchat-message-tool-sink` (`6c80752285`) versus base `main` (`c68484acc4`) — Node + pnpm, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` with external boundaries mocked.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the focused regression test against base `main` (fix absent) and against the PR branch via the repo's vitest runner, then the sibling/contract suite on the branch:
```bash
# RED (PR test on main source) / GREEN (PR branch)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern 'internal source-reply sink|explicit automatic source delivery for webchat' \
  --reporter dot --maxWorkers 1
# Sibling + contract suite (PR branch)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts \
  src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts --reporter dot --maxWorkers 2
```

**Evidence after fix:** Red-green plus a source/contract trace:
- RED on `main` (fix absent): `Tests 2 failed | 2 passed` — *"defaults webchat message tools to the internal source-reply sink"* fails with `AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'message_tool_only'`.
- GREEN on branch: `Test Files 2 passed (2) · Tests 4 passed`.
- Sibling + contract on branch: `Test Files 6 passed (6) · Tests 249 passed (249)` (full `message-tool` + `message-action-runner.send-validation` + `gateway/tool-resolution`).
- Contract: `applyImplicitSourceReplySendPolicy` (`src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts:604`) sets `bestEffort=true` only when `action==="send" && sourceReplyDeliveryMode==="message_tool_only"` and no explicit foreign target — bypassing the `Action send requires a target` throw (`src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.ts:76`). This patch makes internal-channel message tools default to that mode.

**Observed result after fix:** Targetless WebChat `message.send` resolves with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only` and is routed to the internal source-reply sink with no target required. Ordinary WebChat room-event turns remain on `automatic`, and explicit `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` overrides are preserved — both verified by the tests above.

**What was not tested:** A live WebChat send and Control-UI visual capture were out of scope; the repo's gate is focused red-green plus a source/contract trace. The full monorepo vitest suite was not run — coverage was scoped to the affected message-tool module plus the runner send-validation and gateway tool-resolution contracts (249 tests). Non-internal-channel providers are unchanged and exercised by their existing passing tests.

AI assistance disclosure: Codex helped identify the source-sink boundary and implement the patch; the red-green and source/contract proof above were produced with Claude.

target.title: fix(ports): batch macOS lsof listener inspection to one spawn per cycle
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #90548. This is a partial mitigation and intentionally does not close the issue.

This changes Unix listener inspection from one `lsof` spawn per inspected port to one LISTEN snapshot per same-cycle multi-port inspection, then filters the snapshot back into the existing per-port `PortUsage` shape.

Follow-up repairs on current head preserve the pre-existing single-port `inspectPortUsage(port)` command shape and fix the daemon CLI coverage mock for the new batch helper:

- one-port callers stay scoped to `lsof -nP -iTCP:<port> -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`
- explicit multi-port `inspectPortUsages([...])` calls use the shared all-LISTEN snapshot
- `src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts` now exports/mocks `inspectPortUsages`, returns a `Map<number, PortUsage>`, clears it between cases, and asserts the daemon/CLI differing-port status path uses the batch helper

## Affected Surface

- `src/infra/ports-inspect.ts`
  - `readUnixListenersFromLsof`
  - `readUnixListenerSnapshot`
  - `inspectPortUsages`
  - `recordsForPort`
- `src/infra/ports.ts`
  - exports `inspectPortUsages`
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts`
  - batches daemon/CLI port status inspection when the two ports differ
- `src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts`
  - updates the `../infra/ports.js` mock and assertions for the new batch helper

## Scope

Changed:

- Unix LISTEN inspection now supports a shared snapshot command for explicit batch calls:
  - `lsof -nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`
- Single-port Unix LISTEN inspection remains targeted:
  - `lsof -nP -iTCP:<port> -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`
- The snapshot parser preserves pid, command, address, and per-port filtered detail.
- `inspectDaemonPortStatuses` uses the batch helper for daemon/CLI same-cycle checks.
- The daemon CLI coverage mock now includes `inspectPortUsages` so the status path imports and exercises the new named export correctly.
- `checkPortInUse` still runs per port when no matching listener is found.

Intentionally not changed:

- Windows netstat inspection remains separate.
- Linux `ss` fallback remains separate and per-port when lsof fails.
- `inspectPortConnections` / ESTABLISHED connection inspection is untouched.
- The public per-port `PortUsage` result shape is preserved.
- Per-port `detail` does not include unrelated sockets from the all-port lsof snapshot.
- Restart-health polling remains outside this PR, so #90548 should stay open for that remaining surface.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** macOS Unix listener inspection previously spawned `lsof` once per inspected port. The patched multi-port helper inspects multiple same-cycle listener ports with one all-LISTEN `lsof` snapshot and still returns filtered per-port `PortUsage` results. The follow-up repair keeps existing single-port callers targeted to the requested port, so restart-health and other one-port diagnostics do not inherit the all-listener scan. `gateway status` wiring is covered by status collector and daemon CLI coverage regression tests that call `inspectPortUsages([daemonPort, cliPort])` when daemon and CLI ports differ.

**Real environment tested:** local Mac mini4 development machine on `darwin`, branch `wolf/ports-inspect-batch-lsof-listeners`, head `917ef0d4161abc29e93419fb5af560c34b285bb8`. The live proof used two temporary local TCP listeners bound to `127.0.0.1` on ephemeral ports. No secrets, production services, or private traffic were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
# Live macOS behavior proof: start two local TCP listeners, call the real branch helpers,
# and verify both single-port and batch APIs return busy listener details.
node --import tsx --input-type=module

# Focused regression tests and checks on the same head.
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts src/infra/ports-inspect.ts src/infra/ports.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
gitleaks protect --staged --redact
gitleaks git --redact --log-opts origin/main..HEAD
```

**Evidence after fix:** copied live macOS terminal output from current head:

```json
{
  "platform": "darwin",
  "head": "917ef0d4161abc29e93419fb5af560c34b285bb8",
  "ports": [52944, 52945],
  "single": {
    "port": 52944,
    "status": "busy",
    "listenerCount": 1,
    "detailMentionsRequestedPort": true
  },
  "batch": [
    {
      "port": 52944,
      "status": "busy",
      "listenerCount": 1,
      "detailMentionsRequestedPort": true
    },
    {
      "port": 52945,
      "status": "busy",
      "listenerCount": 1,
      "detailMentionsRequestedPort": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Focused validation after fix:

- RED proof before the single-port repair: the new single-port command-shape test failed because the earlier PR head used `-iTCP` instead of `-iTCP:18789`.
- RED proof before the daemon CLI coverage mock repair: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts` failed in `derives probe URL from service args + env (json)` with `__exit__:1` because the mock did not export the newly imported `inspectPortUsages` batch helper.
- GREEN proof after the daemon CLI coverage mock repair: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts` passed, 1 file, 7/7 tests.
- Combined targeted run after the mock repair: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli.coverage.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts` passed, 3 files, 51/51 tests.
- Targeted `oxfmt --check`: all 6 matched files use correct format.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`: passed.
- `gitleaks protect --staged --redact`: no leaks found.
- `gitleaks git --redact --log-opts origin/main..HEAD`: no leaks found.

Command-shape regression coverage:

- `inspectPortUsage(18789)` now asserts exactly one mocked `lsof` invocation with args `-nP -iTCP:18789 -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`.
- `inspectPortUsages([18789, 19001])` still asserts exactly one mocked `lsof` invocation with args `-nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn` and verifies per-port filtering excludes an unrelated port `3000` listener from requested-port details.
- The status collector regression verifies daemon/CLI port status inspection calls `inspectPortUsages([19001, 18789])` and does not call the per-port `inspectPortUsage` path when the ports differ.
- The daemon CLI coverage regression now uses the same batch-helper boundary in its mock and affected assertions.

**Observed result after fix:** on the live macOS proof run, both `inspectPortUsage(52944)` and `inspectPortUsages([52944, 52945])` returned `busy` listener results for real local TCP listeners, with each per-port detail mentioning the requested listener port. Focused tests confirmed the command boundary: single-port checks stay scoped to `-iTCP:<port>`, while explicit multi-port inspection still uses one shared all-LISTEN snapshot. The daemon CLI coverage test now imports the status path successfully and verifies the batch helper is used when daemon and CLI ports differ.

**What was not tested:**

- I did not reproduce launchservicesd / WindowServer saturation. That scenario is disruptive and risks destabilizing the desktop session.
- I did not start or mutate a real OpenClaw LaunchAgent service.
- I did not cover restart-health batching in this PR. Restart-health now keeps its existing targeted single-port lsof behavior; because the remaining repeated health-loop surface is still open, this PR uses `Refs #90548` rather than a closing reference.

## De-duplication Evidence

Immediately before the original push, these five independent open-PR searches returned no results:

- `Fixes #90548`
- `#90548`
- `ports-inspect lsof iTCP`
- `inspectPortUsage readUnixListeners`
- `launchservicesd lsof port`

## Narrow Fix Definition

1. This is a shared helper/default-policy gap: the Unix port-inspection helper owned a per-port process-spawn strategy.
2. Fixing only one visible caller would leave adjacent status/diagnostic paths able to repeat the same per-port lsof pattern, so the fix is helper-level plus batch-aware status caller wiring.
3. Maintainers should expect a canonical shared helper fix rather than a local status-only patch because the bug is process-spawn strategy, not output formatting.
4. Supersession risk is lower with this shared boundary: a narrower macOS-app-only or status-only patch would be more likely to be replaced.

target.title: chore(codeowners): gate heartbeat template and repair changes
target.body:
## Summary

Add CODEOWNERS review gates for heartbeat template, content emptiness check, template resolution, and doctor repair logic. Changes to any of these files now require review from @hxy91819 or @vincentkoc.

### Scope

| File | Role | Risk if changed incorrectly |
|---|---|---|
| `src/agents/templates/HEARTBEAT.md` | Runtime heartbeat template content | Non-comment lines in template cause heartbeat API calls to fire for all newly bootstrapped workspaces |
| `src/agents/workspace-templates.ts` | Template directory resolution and search paths | Changes to `FALLBACK_TEMPLATE_DIR` or candidate order could redirect heartbeat template loading back to docs directory, breaking the decoupling from #85416 |
| `src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts` | `isHeartbeatContentEffectivelyEmpty()` and `parseHeartbeatTasks()` | Changes to emptiness check logic can cause false skips (heartbeat not firing) or false triggers (unnecessary API calls) |
| `src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.ts` | Doctor repair matching and fix logic | Hardcoded constants are coupled to template content; mismatches cause missed detections or false `dirty-template-with-custom-content` verdicts |

### Background

Historically, the heartbeat runtime template (`HEARTBEAT.md`) was loaded from the docs template directory (`docs/reference/templates/`). Docs edits (frontmatter, fenced code blocks, prose descriptions, "Related" sections) leaked into user workspace files after `stripFrontMatter`, causing:

- Non-comment/non-header lines (prose, markdown links like `- [Heartbeat config](...)`) made `isHeartbeatContentEffectivelyEmpty()` return `false`, triggering heartbeat API calls for users who never configured any tasks.
- This happened multiple times across different docs template versions (2026-01, 2026-03, 2026-04), resulting in numerous user complaints about unexpected heartbeat activity and API token consumption.

PR #85416 decoupled the runtime template to `src/agents/templates/HEARTBEAT.md`, preventing docs edits from affecting runtime behavior. However, the risk remains: if any of the gated files are modified incorrectly, the same class of regression reoccurs.

### Purpose

The purpose of this CODEOWNERS gate is to ensure that any change to these files triggers a required review discussion. A seemingly harmless edit can cause all newly bootstrapped workspaces to fire heartbeat API calls unexpectedly, leading to significant unintended token consumption for users. The designated reviewers can evaluate whether the change is safe and whether the coupled code (doctor repair constants, emptiness check logic, template resolution paths) needs to be updated in tandem.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- CODEOWNERS syntax verified against GitHub documentation (last-match-wins semantics, user/team references)

target.title: fix: recover stuck Control UI chat runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Repairs #42258 as the canonical fix path for #42213.
- Keeps the stuck-run recovery scoped to the Control UI chat lifecycle and queued-message flush behavior.
- Addresses hydrated Greptile findings: do not surface transient background probe failures as user-visible errors, avoid double watchdog scheduling, and remove redundant run-activity writes.

## Credit

target.title: feat(slack): add workspace member policy
target.body:
## Summary

- add `channels.slack.memberPolicy` so Slack can fail closed to full workspace members before dispatch
- cache `users.info` access metadata and apply the policy to normal inbound messages plus Slack system/interactive events
- document the new config and cover schema, account merge, and policy decisions with tests

## Policy behavior

When enabled, the policy defaults to:

- require the sender `team_id` to match the workspace returned by `auth.test`
- deny restricted and ultra-restricted guests
- deny Slack Connect/external strangers
- deny bot/app users
- deny deleted users
- fail closed if `users.info` cannot verify the sender

Example:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "slack": {
      "memberPolicy": {
        "enabled": true,
        "denyGuests": true,
        "denyExternal": true,
        "denyBots": true
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Slack inbound authorization can now be restricted to real full members of the connected workspace instead of allowing guests, Slack Connect strangers, bots/apps, deleted users, or users from another team through the normal Slack message/action dispatch path.

Real environment tested: A live OpenClaw installation connected to a production Slack workspace through the Slack plugin. User IDs and names are redacted; only aggregate counts are included.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live Slack directory command and evaluated the returned Slack user metadata with this PR's default `memberPolicy` decisions (`team_id` match, deny deleted users, bots/apps, restricted/ultra-restricted guests, and Slack Connect strangers).

```bash
openclaw directory peers list --channel slack --json
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted live output from the real OpenClaw/Slack setup:

```json
{
  "checkedAt": "2026-06-05T09:44:06.230Z",
  "expectedTeam": "T026RK84A",
  "total": 454,
  "allowedWorkspaceHumans": 46,
  "denied": {
    "deleted": 165,
    "bot": 102,
    "guest": 141,
    "external": 0,
    "teamMismatch": 0,
    "missingTeam": 0
  },
  "sampleAllowedIds": [
    "redacted",
    "redacted",
    "redacted",
    "redacted",
    "redacted"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The policy keeps 46 full workspace humans eligible and excludes 141 guest accounts plus non-human/deleted Slack directory entries from the real workspace. No external/Slack Connect strangers or team mismatches were present in this workspace at the time of the live check.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/slack/src/monitor/member-policy.test.ts extensions/slack/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/slack/src/accounts.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`

target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Agents have workspace directories but no way to browse or manage files through the Control UI.
- Why it matters: Users need to inspect, edit, and organize agent workspace files without SSH or CLI access.
- What changed: Added `agents.workspace.*` gateway RPC methods (list, get, set, delete, mkdir, move, stat) and a Workspace tab in the agents panel with file browser, editor, and file operations.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to agent runtime behavior, plugin SDK, or existing file/config panels.

## Change Type (select all)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61368

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agents-workspace.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Path traversal rejection, CRUD operations, file size limits, error handling
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit tests cover all API methods with mocked fs, validating security and correctness without requiring a running gateway
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None (new feature)
- If no new test is added, why not: Tests are included

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New "Workspace" tab in the agents panel for browsing and managing agent workspace files
- File browser with directory navigation, breadcrumbs, and sorting (directories first)
- Inline file editor with dirty state tracking and save/delete actions
- File upload, download, and mkdir operations
- 10MB file size limit enforced server-side

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user] -> [agents panel] -> [overview | files | tools | skills | channels | cron]

After:
[user] -> [agents panel] -> [overview | files | workspace | tools | skills | channels | cron]
                                          |
                                    [file tree] + [file editor]
                                          |
                              agents.workspace.* RPC -> fs-safe
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes - workspace file read/write through gateway RPC
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes - new `agents.workspace.*` RPC methods
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? Yes - workspace directory access scoped per agent
- Risk + mitigation: All file operations use `fs-safe` helpers with path traversal protection. Relative paths are validated to reject `..` and absolute paths. File size capped at 10MB. Operations are scoped to the resolved agent workspace directory.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Docker (openclaw-workspace-test:latest)
- Model/provider: N/A (UI feature)
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: Default gateway config with `--allow-unconfigured --bind lan`

### Steps

1. Start gateway with `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured`
2. Open Control UI and navigate to an agent
3. Click the "Workspace" tab
4. Browse files, select a file to edit, modify content, click Save

### Expected

- File tree shows workspace contents with correct icons and sizes
- Editing a file enables the Save button (dirty state)
- Clicking Save persists changes and disables the button
- Navigating away resets editor state

### Actual

- All operations work as expected

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Unit tests: 40+ test cases covering security (path traversal), CRUD, edge cases, and error handling all pass.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: File browse, edit, save, delete, mkdir, navigate, upload via Docker container with mounted ~/.openclaw
- Edge cases checked: Path traversal attempts, empty directories, large file rejection, file selection reset on navigate
- What I did not verify: Base64 encoding upload through UI, symlink display

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: New RPC surface increases attack surface for path traversal
  - Mitigation: All paths validated with `validateRelativePath()` rejecting `..` and absolute paths, plus `fs-safe` helpers enforce root directory containment

target.title: docs: require i18n postprocess before skip
target.body:
## Summary
- require docs-i18n postprocess completion before localized outputs become skippable
- mark newly written localized docs as `postprocess_version: pending` and finalize them only after locale-link postprocess succeeds
- add regression coverage for the sticky-failure path where stale English links could otherwise survive retries

## Root cause
- docs-i18n writes translated localized files before `postprocessLocalizedDocs(...)` runs
- those files already carried a fresh `x-i18n.source_hash`, so a later postprocess failure could leave stale English-root links on disk
- on retry with `--overwrite=false`, `shouldSkipDoc(...)` only looked at `source_hash`, so the broken file could be skipped indefinitely unless the English source changed

## Fix
- `encodeFrontMatter(...)` now writes `x-i18n.postprocess_version: pending`
- `postprocessLocalizedDocs(...)` upgrades that marker to the current postprocess version only after it rewrites or finalizes the file
- `shouldSkipDoc(...)` now requires both a matching `source_hash` and the current postprocess version before it skips a localized file

## Test plan
- `go test ./...` in `scripts/docs-i18n`
